1.texto_original: DAVIDE adunque, essendo vecchio, e sazio di giorni, costituì Salomone, suo figliuolo, re sopra Israele.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 23:1

"DAVIDE adunque, essendo vecchio, e sazio di giorni, costituì Salomone, suo figliuolo, re sopra Israele."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Cronache 23:1

Philological Analysis

"DAVIDE adunque, essendo vecchio, e sazio di giorni, costituì Salomone, suo figliuolo, re sopra Israele."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"DAVIDE adunque, essendo vecchio, e sazio di giorni, costituì Salomone, suo figliuolo, re sopra Israele."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

30.texto_original: e per presentarsi ogni mattina, per celebrare, e lodare il Signore; e così ogni sera;
30.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 23:30

"e per presentarsi ogni mattina, per celebrare, e lodare il Signore; e così ogni sera;"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
30.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of 1 Cronache 23:30

Philological Analysis

"e per presentarsi ogni mattina, per celebrare, e lodare il Signore; e così ogni sera;"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
30.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"e per presentarsi ogni mattina, per celebrare, e lodare il Signore; e così ogni sera;"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

31.texto_original: ed ogni volta che si aveano da offerire olocausti al Signore, ne’ sabati, nelle calendi, nelle feste solenni; in certo numero, secondo ch’era ordinato del continuo, davanti al Signore;
31.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 23:31

"ed ogni volta che si aveano da offerire olocausti al Signore, ne’ sabati, nelle calendi, nelle feste solenni; in certo numero, secondo ch’era ordinato del continuo, davanti al Signore;"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.

32.texto_original: e per osservar ciò che si dovea fare nel Tabernacolo della convenenza, e nel santuario, e per lo servigio de’ figliuoli di Aaronne, lor fratelli, per lo ministerio della Casa del Signore.
32.normal:
Devotional Study of 1 Cronache 23:32

"e per osservar ciò che si dovea fare nel Tabernacolo della convenenza, e nel santuario, e per lo servigio de’ figliuoli di Aaronne, lor fratelli, per lo ministerio della Casa del Signore."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
